## News Summary
- Gold rose in Asia on March 2, 2026 as Middle East tensions sustained safe-haven demand. The Guardian reports gold up about 2% in early Asia trade, near $5,397 per ounce. This aligns with a broader safety-buy mood amid US-Israeli strikes on Iran. ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/mar/02/oil-prices-iran-war-strait-of-hormuz-shipping?utm_source=openai))
- On March 1, 2026, analysts noted a potential move higher for gold due to US-Iran tensions. New Straits Times quotes SPI Asset Management’s Stephen Innes suggesting gold could reach around $5,500/oz on the flare-up. ([nst.com.my](https://www.nst.com.my/amp/business/economy/2026/03/1387533/gold-may-hit-us5500-ounce-us-iran-tensions-says-analyst?utm_source=openai))
- Live updates from India’s Livemint on March 1, 2026 describe spot gold hovering above $5,200/oz, with safe-haven demand supporting prices near $5,278/oz in that session. ([livemint.com](https://www.livemint.com/market/stock-market-news/gold-silver-price-today-live-march-1-2026-us-iran-war-impact-gold-2-lakh-prediction-silver-100-dollars-mcx-opening-11772357102180.html?utm_source=openai))
- AFP/NAMPA coverage from Tokyo on March 2, 2026 notes gold trading around the mid-$5,300s in Asia, e.g., about $5,384.52/oz, as tensions persisted. ([nampa.org](https://www.nampa.org/text/22873718?utm_source=openai))

Notes:
- The above sources reflect price activity within the March 1–2, 2026 window and describe the prevailing market mood as safe-haven buying amid geopolitical risk. Prices cited are representative across major markets and might differ slightly by exact quote source and time.

Economic calendar and events (today’s/this period)
- From the MQL5 Economic Calendar (covering March 1–2, 2026), key events that could influence XAUUSD include:
  - 2026-03-02 15:00 UTC: USD ISM Manufacturing PMI (Actual not disclosed here; forecasts shown as 53.0 with previous 52.6). Also 15:00 UTC: ISM Manufacturing Prices Paid (Forecast 62.2; Prev 59.0), ISM Manufacturing Employment (Forecast 45.4; Prev 48.1), and ISM Manufacturing New Orders (Forecast 57.8; Prev 57.1). These U.S. data releases tend to move gold when the data surprise versus expectations. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
  - 2026-03-02 14:00–14:45 UTC: S&P Global/PMI readings for Europe (e.g., 14:00 EUR S&P Global Manufacturing PMI; 14:45 USD S&P Global Manufacturing PMI; forecasts shown, with actuals typically disclosed at release). These influence risk sentiment and dollar dynamics, indirectly affecting XAUUSD. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
  - Central bank speeches anticipated: 14:00 UTC ECB President Lagarde Speech and 14:00 UTC ECB President Lagarde or Nagel appearances (ECB governance speakers listed on the page). Also 09:15 UTC EUR Elderson Speech. These can impact euro-dynamics and dollar outlook, thus gold. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
  - Other regional PMI releases and BoE-related figures later on March 2 (e.g., BoE Ramsden speech at 15:30 UTC) and various manufacturing PMI updates. While not gold-specific, they shape global risk appetite and currency moves that affect XAUUSD. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))

Analyst forecasts (near-term to 2026)
- Morgan Stanley has signaled higher gold prices for 2026, with forecasts around the mid-4,000s to near $4,800/oz by late 2026 (e.g., a Reuters note cited by Morgan Stanley in early January 2026). This reflects a bullish multi‑month view supported by central-bank demand and macro uncertainty. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/commodities-news/morgan-stanley-forecasts-gold-at-4800-by-fourth-quarter-of-2026-4432140?utm_source=openai))
- Morgan Stanley’s earlier note (January 6, 2026) suggested a rally to about $4,800/oz by Q4 2026, driven by falling real yields, potential Fed policy changes, and ongoing central-bank buying. ([investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/commodities-news/morgan-stanley-forecasts-gold-at-4800-by-fourth-quarter-of-2026-4432140?utm_source=openai))
- New Straits Times, March 1, 2026, quotes SPI Asset Management’s Stephen Innes arguing gold could hit around $5,500/oz amid US-Iran tensions, i.e., a near-term bullish scenario based on geopolitical risk. ([nst.com.my](https://www.nst.com.my/amp/business/economy/2026/03/1387533/gold-may-hit-us5500-ounce-us-iran-tensions-says-analyst?utm_source=openai))

Current price context

Independent market reports from March 1–2, 2026 place spot gold in the roughly $5,200–$5,400/oz band (with some reports around $5,380–$5,400 and others near $5,278 or $5,397). Price quotes vary by source and timing, which is common in fast-moving safe-haven markets. ([livemint.com](https://www.livemint.com/market/stock-market-news/gold-silver-price-today-live-march-1-2026-us-iran-war-impact-gold-2-lakh-prediction-silver-100-dollars-mcx-opening-11772357102180.html?utm_source=openai))

In addition, analyst commentary cited in early March points to continued upside in gold given ongoing geopolitical risk and central-bank buying. ([theguardian.com](https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/mar/02/oil-prices-iran-war-strait-of-hormuz-shipping?utm_source=openai))

## News Summary
- Economic calendar and central-bank activity shaping EURUSD today (Mar 2, 2026):
  - ECB-related communications on Mar 2: ECB Executive Board member Elderson is slated to speak at 09:15 CET, and ECB President Christine Lagarde is slated to speak at 14:00 CET. There is also a mention of a Bundesbank official (Nagel) speaking around the same day in some calendars. These events can influence euro direction ahead of euro-area data releases. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
  - Euro-area data and events on Mar 2: Retail sales (EUR) at 07:00 CET (m/m and y/y) and S&P Global Manufacturing PMIs for the euro area around the morning, followed by a Bundesbank-related event. There are also euro-area government debt instrument auctions (3M/6M/12M BTF) around 13:50 CET. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
  - Balancing euro-area policy rhetoric: A speech by Burkhard Balz (a Bundesbank official) occurred/was scheduled for Mar 2, 2026, touching on European financial-system resilience and digital monetary policy considerations. This can inform expectations for EUR/USD moves. ([bundesbank.de](https://www.bundesbank.de/en/press/speeches?utm_source=openai))
- Central-bank communications context (relevant for EURUSD moves in the window):
  - Deutsche Bundesbank and ECB-related communications are prominent this week, with multiple speeches and comments that investors will parse for clues on policy stance and the euro’s path. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))

Notes:
- The above lines reflect items listed on the official Economic Calendar page you asked me to review (EUR-focused items are shown under the EUR column and include Nagel/Lagarde/Elderson speeches and EUR data). ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))

## Sentiment
- Market mood appears to be cautious/neutral heading into a data-heavy and speech-driven session. The EUR is at a crossroads as traders weigh upcoming PMI releases and multiple high-profile speeches (Lagarde, Elderson) against the backdrop of ongoing euro-area data flow and potential ECB guidance on policy. The presence of several euro-area data prints and central-bank remarks on Mar 2 tends to keep EURUSD range-bound until a clear policy stance or stronger data signal emerges. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
- Additionally, the Balz speech on Mar 2 underscores ongoing focus on European financial stability and digital-euro considerations, which can inject a nuanced euro-positive tilt if policy signals are constructive. ([bundesbank.de](https://www.bundesbank.de/en/press/speeches?utm_source=openai))

## AI RECOMMENDATION: NEUTRAL
- Reasoning: The current window is dominated by scheduled ECB-related speeches and euro-area data (PMIs, retail sales, and auctions). Without a clear, data-driven breakout in the PMI or a hawkish/dovish surprise in Lagarde/Elderson remarks, EURUSD is likely to remain within a familiar range near current levels. The mixed signals from euro-area data and ECB commentary imply no strong directional impulse at this moment. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
- Supporting context: Analyst forecasts from major banks suggest a broad-range, longer-term euro strength scenarios (e.g., forecasts around 1.20–1.25 by year-end from large banks). While these are longer-horizon views, they reflect underlying euro-positive sentiment in some camps, which could be revisited if ECB policy or euro-area data surprises tilt the narrative. Examples include Goldman Sachs/UBS/JPMorgan-style targets cited in market commentary. These become relevant only if data/policy diverges from the current path. ([fxempire.com](https://www.fxempire.com/forecasts/article/eurusd-forecast-2026-outlook-points-to-extended-euro-strength-ahead-1568596?utm_source=openai))
- Confidence Level: MEDIUM

Cited sources (highlights):
- MQL5 Economic Calendar (EUR events, times, and central-bank speeches for Mar 1–2, 2026): shows 2026.03.01 15:00 Bbk Nagel Speech; 2026.03.02 09:15 Elderson Speech; 2026.03.02 14:00 Lagarde Speech; 2026.03.02 14:00 Nagel Speech; 2026.03.02 07:00/08:15/08:45/08:55 EUR PMI and 07:00 EUR Retail Sales; 13:50 EUR Auctions. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
- Bundesbank Speeches (Balz—Future-proofing Europe’s financial system: Innovation, stability, and sovereignty—Brussels, 03.02.2026): confirms a March 2 central-bank communications context. ([bundesbank.de](https://www.bundesbank.de/en/press/speeches?utm_source=openai))
- Lagarde/euro-area sentiment context (ECB-related commentary and euro strength considerations from credible outlets): provides background on how ECB communications can influence EUR strength. ([euronews.com](https://www.euronews.com/business/2026/02/05/ecb-rates-unchanged-lagarde-inflation-is-in-a-good-place?utm_source=openai))

If you’d like, I can:
- Pull in additional March 1–2, 2026 EURUSD-specific headlines from outlets like Reuters, Bloomberg, or FX-focused sites, if available.
- Monitor the actual releases and update the summary with real-time reactions to the PMI prints and speeches.

ai_web_research.ai_response:
## News Summary
- On Mar 2, 2026, the focus for GBP/USD is likely to come from UK economic data and Bank of England communications rather than a flood of breaking headlines. The key GBP-related items scheduled for the window include:
  - Nationwide House Price Index (HPI) data for February 2026 (MoM and YoY) due at 07:00 GMT. Forecasts: MoM +0.2%, YoY +1.2%. This is the UK housing-price data that can influence sterling if outcomes surprise. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
  - Bank of England data releases (Consumer Credit, M4 Money Supply, Mortgage Approvals, Mortgage Lending, Net Lending to Individuals) at 09:30 GMT, with forecasts published (examples: Consumer Credit +£1.29B, M4 Money Supply +0.2%, Mortgage Approvals ~61.98K, Mortgage Lending +£4.60B, Net Lending to Individuals +£6.16B). These are part of the domestic demand backdrop and can sway GBPUSD intraday. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
  - S&P Global/CIPS UK Manufacturing PMI (Feb) at 09:30 GMT, forecast 52.0 (unchanged from prior). This provides a snapshot of manufacturing health and can influence BoE expectations. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
  - BoE Deputy Governor Ramsden is scheduled to speak at 15:30 GMT, a potential source of guidance on the BoE’s policy stance and the pace of any future easing. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
  - ECB-related events are on the calendar (ECB President Lagarde speech at 14:00 GMT and an Elderson speech at 09:15 GMT). While not GBP-specific, they can impact risk sentiment and the euro/sterling cross. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
- In addition, US data (ISM Manufacturing PMI and related prices) is on the calendar for 15:00 GMT, which can drive dollar moves and, by extension, the GBP/USD pair in this window. Forecasts include ISM Manufacturing PMI at 53.0 and related components. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))

Notes:
- The above items are the scheduled events visible in the March 2, 2026 window on the MQL5 Economic Calendar. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
- Next-release context (e.g., Nationwide HPI timing) is supported by industry calendars indicating a March 2, 2026 timing for the February data. See Nationwide/industry calendars for March 2, 2026 timing. ([mtsinsights.com](https://www.mtsinsights.com/events/4363/?utm_source=openai))

Current price reference you gave: 1.34251 USD per GBP. The events above are the inputs likely to move that level in the window you asked for.

## Sentiment
- Likely to be range-bound to modestly range-extending in the near term. UK domestic data (HPI, consumer credit, mortgage data, PMI) are expected to come in at or near forecast, which could keep the pound in a narrow range around the 1.34–1.36 area unless a surprise drives a quick shift in BoE expectations. The presence of Ramsden’s speech adds a real-time risk for a sentiment shift depending on tone. Overall mood: neutral-to-cautiously constructive for GBP if UK data beat expectations; otherwise, consolidation or mild selling pressure if data disappoints and BoE easing bets rise. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))

## AI RECOMMENDATION: NEUTRAL
**Reasoning**: 
- The immediate driver set for Mar 2 is a pack of UK data releases and a BoE speech, with forecasts broadly in line with prior trends. There’s no clear, breaking GBP-positive or GBP-negative catalyst in the window from major headlines, based on the search results and the official calendar. The current price sits near the lower end of a short-term range, and unless a data surprise or a hawkish/clear tone from Ramsden shifts the BoE expectations, the trend is likely to stay range-bound in the 1.34–1.36 zone. The broader global backdrop (ECB speak, US ISM data) could add volatility but isn’t signaling a decisive directional breakout in this narrow window. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
- Analyst forecasts around this horizon are mixed and depend on policy paths for BoE vs. Fed, with some institutions signaling higher GBP levels if UK easing is further delayed, while others expect firmer dollar dynamics to cap GBP strength. Examples include JPMorgan/Morgan Stanley/MUFG-type views cited in market analyses (e.g., JPM: ~1.39 by early 2026; Morgan Stanley: ~1.47 by year-end; MUFG: ~1.40 mid-2026). These are not guaranteed outcomes and depend on data surprises. ([fxempire.com](https://www.fxempire.com/forecasts/article/gbp-usd-forecast-2026-sterling-faces-limits-but-outlook-improves-1568579?utm_source=openai))
**Confidence Level**: MEDIUM

If you’d like, I can broaden the search to include 48–72 hours around Mar 1–2, 2026 for more “latest news” headlines (Reuters/Bloomberg/FT/CNBC) and pull in any intraday price moves or quotes that show the actual numbers from the data releases as they come out.

- What is available from the date window are scheduled economic events and central-bank-related releases that could drive USD/JPY moves:
  - US ISM Manufacturing data due on March 2, 2026 at 15:00 UTC (ISM Manufacturing PMI, Prices Paid, Employment, New Orders are all on the calendar with forecasts around mid-50s to low-60s for various components). ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
  - Bank of Japan data due on March 2, 2026 at 23:30–23:50 UTC (BoJ Monetary Base y/y is listed; unemployment and capital spending data for Japan are also scheduled nearby). These releases can influence the yen and USD/JPY volatility. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))

Notes:
- Current price provided: 156.766 (as of your message). I did not find a March 1–2, 2026 news article confirming a new high/low in that window; the most actionable items appear to be calendar releases rather than explicit news stories. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))

## Sentiment
- Neutral to cautious. Without a confirmed USD/JPY news headline in the March 1–2 window, the near-term direction will hinge on the outcome of the US ISM data (favoring the USD if stronger-than-expected) versus BoJ base money data (which could strengthen the Yen if the readings hint at policy normalization). The calendar suggests potential for heightened volatility around those releases, rather than a clear, sustained move in one direction. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))

## AI RECOMMENDATION: NEUTRAL
**Reasoning**: 
- The available March 1–2, 2026 window shows planned US and Japanese data/events rather than a definitive USDJPY news catalyst. The US ISM PMI and BoJ monetary-base metrics are the key potential movers; results could push USDJPY either higher (if US data surprises to the upside) or lower (if BoJ data or policy signals shift). In the absence of a posted, credible USDJPY-specific news headline within the window, and given the price around 156.77, there is no robust, single-direction signal. The consensus from calendar data points to data-driven volatility rather than a clear trend. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))

**Confidence Level**: MEDIUM

Additional context and sources you might find useful:
- MQL5 Economic Calendar page (shows the March 1–2, 2026 schedule, including US ISM Manufacturing PMI on March 2 at 15:00 UTC and BoJ/Japanese data later that day). This is the best structured source for the exact events within your date window. ([mql5.com](https://www.mql5.com/en/economic-calendar))
- Forecasts/analyst language around USD/JPY around this period can be found in aggregators (e.g., ExchangeRates.org.uk) that published updated forecasts around March 1, 2026. Keep in mind these are consensus/aggregated forecasts and should be treated as supplementary rather than definitive market signals. Example updated forecast page dated Mar 1, 2026.
